summary: The pytest smoke test run crashed with a fatal segmentation fault while executing tests in numpy/_core/tests/test_datetime.py. The Python process was killed mid-run, preventing any further test collection or reporting. Earlier meson build probe errors are normal feature-detection noise and did not prevent the build from completing.
root_cause: A segmentation fault in the CPython interpreter occurred during execution of numpy/_core/tests/test_datetime.py, aborting the entire test process.

evidence.reason: Explicit fatal crash message emitted by CPython when the process receives SIGSEGV, confirming the test run was aborted by a segfault rather than a normal test assertion failure.
The segfault occurred partway through test_datetime.py (after 13 passing dots), pinpointing the crashing file and confirming tests were running before the crash.
All tests in test__exceptions.py passed cleanly, confirming the build and import machinery work and the crash is isolated to test_datetime.py execution.

suggested_fix: Reproduce locally by running `python -m pytest numpy/_core/tests/test_datetime.py -x` under Python 3.12 with a debug build or ASAN enabled to identify which specific test and C-level call site triggers the segfault, then bisect recent changes to datetime-related C code.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
